summary: DHH announces Rails 0.9, a major release with close to 100 changes including a faster development environment, runtime breakpoints for inspecting applications, and a new validation DSL. The release caches the framework while allowing application reloading under WEBrick and FastCGI. He highlights the community contributions from the #rubyonrails IRC channel as essential to making the release possible.
key_points: Rails 0.9 introduces a faster development environment that caches the framework while reloading application code
New breakpoint feature allows inspecting and modifying an application mid-execution
Validation system extends Rails' domain language with new declarative words
The release was driven by community contributors from the #rubyonrails IRC channel
money_quotes: Major is also the new breakpoints that allows you to inspect an application during execution, change the model, and resume.
We got a whole new way to do validations that extends the domain language of Rails with a wide range of new words.
This massive release would never have been possible without the killer community hanging out at #rubyonrails.
They really showed what a dedicated group of programmers are capable of.
key_insight: Rails 0.9 marked a pivotal early release where community-driven development produced a massive set of developer experience improvements, from faster reloading to runtime breakpoints, establishing the collaborative open-source model that would define Rails.
